pi_items.source: setup_openbci.sh line 18 creates /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots directory; export_result.sh line 9 defines expected screenshot location

OpenBCI official documentation (docs.openbci.com): Cyton+Daisy board configuration supports 16-channel capacity. Documentation states: 'The OpenBCI Daisy Module is a daughter card to the Cyton Board, and expands the number of sensor channels from 8 to 16.'

OpenBCI official documentation (docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/): GUI supports SYNTHETIC data source which generates algorithmic test data. Source is available in GUI data source menu options.

privileged_info_summary: Task configures OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 to simulate a 16-channel Cyton+Daisy board setup using the SYNTHETIC (PC) data source which generates algorithmic test waveforms. Users must select SYNTHETIC data source, set channel count to 16 channels, start the session, verify all 16 channels appear in the Time Series widget with active waveforms, and save a screenshot to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/16ch_synthetic_session.png. All data is purely synthetic, not from real hardware. Channel count of 16 is verified by OpenBCI official documentation as the standard configuration for Cyton+Daisy boards.
